raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX XXXX XXXX, I received notification from Citi Cards that someone was using my Best Buy Credit Card Fraudulently. At the time I received the text message I was sitting in the Dentist chair so I could not get back to them until a bit later. I called them and let them know that none of the charges they told me went through there were XXXX total were mine. The second one XXXX the agent said was XXXX for {$1000.00}. All of the other charges have been removed except this one and it is still showing up on my statement. At the time of the fraud, I owed a balance of {$600.00}, as I had purchased something on XX/XX/2025, and was told I could either get points or take 18 months to pay the balance off in full. This is the only amount I owe. I have called Citi CardsXXXX XXXX XXXX  on the following dates XX/XX/2025, XX/XX/2025, XX/XX/2025, XX/XX/2025, and attempted again today XX/XX/2025, but the automated service kept cutting me off. I pulled the statement for this month today so I could get the bill paid and it is still showing the fraudulent purchase on my account, and I do not owe this. I also don't want this to affect my credit score ; I have a perfect credit score and this extra {$1000.00} on my account will lower it. I am planning on moving and need my score to remain perfect, so I don't have any issues. Trying to get anyone at Citi Cards to do anything is ridiculous and unfortunately more times than not when I have spoken to the associates they don't understand or speak XXXX. I don't know what else to do at this point because although I have told them numerous times the {$1000.00} is fraud, they are NOT removing this. Not only are they not removing it but these charges were posted and made XXXX day after the initial fraud, and I was told that the card had been frozen so no more charges could be made. Yet this {$1000.00} shows on the statement as XXXX. I need help and Citi Card, Best Buy is not doing anything to help. 
Best Regards, XXXX XXXX
